title: +## Plexus and Hypergraph Control
content:
+## Plexus and Hypergraph Control
+
+Conventional NLP pipelines impose a strict sequence of operations that cannot accommodate the complexity of human communication【33635101037775†L33-L68】. RLOS instead implements a **plexus**: a dynamic network in which modules send queries and constraints to one another. The central data structure is a **unified hypergraph** that stores multimodal representations; each module reads from and writes to this hypergraph【33635101037775†L253-L272】. The hypergraph is continuously updated as modules refine interpretations; for example, the pragmatic module may prompt the syntactic parser to re‑evaluate an ambiguous structure【33635101037775†L279-L284】.
+
+This plexus is governed by a *meta‑learning controller* that monitors performance, allocates resources and resolves conflicts. It supports **RAW** and **PUBLIC** modes. In RAW mode, analysis and generation are unrestricted, providing full access for research. PUBLIC mode inserts safety gates for truthfulness, bias mitigation and cultural sensitivity【3†L123-L145】.
+
+The following diagram (adapted from the AI responses) illustrates the **RLOS flow**: user input passes through mode selection, layered analysis and cross‑modal integration; outputs are filtered through cultural analysis, safety gates and adaptation loops, and user feedback feeds into system evolution.
